description: Lock Roman road-administration cleanup so mapping and customs nodes do not regain narrower transport-funding dependencies.

checks.reason: Standardized road milestones are anchored to regular Roman milestone practice after 124 BCE.
Road maintenance administration is anchored to Augustus's curatores viarum reforms in 20 BCE.
The node is rescoped to Roman road-maintenance funding, not generic ancient toll roads.
Road maps and itineraries can record routes without requiring later standardized milestone infrastructure.
Customs checkpoints are tariff institutions, not downstream products of Roman road-maintenance funding.
